name: code-refactoring-context-restore
description: Use when working with code refactoring context restore
capabilities: Provide semantic vector search strategies for context retrieval
Guide context rehydration with token budget management
Offer workflow patterns for project resumption after interruptions
Explain context merging and conflict resolution techniques
Detail session state reconstruction for multi-agent workflows
Deliver relevance filtering and ranking best practices
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 16 external_commands patterns and 2 weak_crypto patterns. Evaluation reveals these are FALSE POSITIVES: The external_commands detections are markdown code examples (e.g., `context-restore project:ai-assistant --mode full`) used as documentation, not actual shell execution. The weak_crypto flags are references to cryptographic concepts in documentation text, not actual crypto implementation. This is a documentation/guide skill containing only instructional content and example code snippets.
valueStatement: This skill helps AI assistants recover and reconstruct project context across complex refactoring sessions, ensuring continuity and minimal information loss when resuming long-running code improvement tasks.
limitations: Does not execute actual context restoration operations
Requires external vector database and storage systems
Cannot access project files without explicit user permission
Provides guidance only, not direct code execution

useCases.targetUser: Developers working on large-scale refactoring projects with intermittent progress
useCases.title: Resume Interrupted Refactoring Sessions
useCases.description: When returning to a complex refactoring project after days or weeks, use this skill to reconstruct the full context including architectural decisions, completed changes, and pending tasks.

useCases.targetUser: Development teams onboarding new developers or AI assistants
useCases.title: Onboard AI to Existing Codebase
useCases.description: New team members or fresh AI sessions can quickly understand project structure, technology decisions, and current development priorities through semantic context retrieval.

useCases.targetUser: Senior developers and architects working on multiple related projects
useCases.title: Cross-Project Knowledge Transfer
useCases.description: Transfer relevant architectural patterns and decisions from one project to another by extracting and adapting semantic context vectors.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Context Recovery
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to restore context for my code refactoring project. The project uses [language/framework]. Can you guide me through retrieving semantic context including architectural decisions and recent changes?
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to resume a refactoring session with basic context retrieval

promptTemplates.title: Token-Optimized Restoration
promptTemplates.prompt: My context window is limited. Help me prioritize which context components to restore first. I have a [X] token budget and need to cover [specific areas].
promptTemplates.scenario: User needs efficient context restoration within strict token limits

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Agent Context Sync
promptTemplates.prompt: I am working with multiple AI agents on the same refactoring task. How should I structure context so each agent has consistent understanding of project state and decision history?
promptTemplates.scenario: Team using multiple AI assistants for collaborative refactoring

promptTemplates.title: Conflict Resolution in Merged Context
promptTemplates.prompt: I have two context snapshots from different sessions that may have conflicting information about the codebase. What strategies can I use to detect and resolve these conflicts?
promptTemplates.scenario: User dealing with conflicting context from parallel sessions

bestPractices: Always validate restored context against current codebase state before making changes
Use incremental context loading to stay within token budgets while maintaining accuracy
Implement cryptographic signatures for context integrity verification
antiPatterns: Restoring full historical context without relevance filtering - leads to token overflow
Ignoring temporal decay in context ranking - outdated information gets priority
Assuming context is current without validating against live codebase state

faq.question: What is semantic context restoration?
faq.answer: Semantic context restoration is the process of recovering project knowledge, architectural decisions, and development history using vector-based semantic search and intelligent ranking algorithms.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code?
faq.answer: No. This is a guide and documentation skill. It provides best practices, workflows, and example code snippets but does not directly execute any operations.

faq.question: What token budget should I use for context restoration?
faq.answer: A token budget of 8192 is recommended as default. Adjust based on your AI model's context window and project complexity. Prioritize critical components like architecture and recent changes.

faq.question: Can this work with any programming language?
faq.answer: Yes. The strategies and workflows are language-agnostic. The skill provides guidance applicable to any codebase, with examples spanning Python, Ruby, JavaScript, and other languages.

faq.question: How do I handle conflicting context from multiple sessions?
faq.answer: Use three-way merge strategies: (1) Identify conflicting components; (2) Apply semantic similarity to determine authoritative source; (3) Preserve provenance metadata for traceability.

faq.question: What is the relevance threshold for semantic search?
faq.answer: A relevance threshold of 0.75 (75% cosine similarity) is recommended. Lower values include more context but reduce precision. Higher values improve accuracy but may miss relevant components.
